findings.description: Header/logo area is not preserved in the same visual form.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the USDA logo and department name as a compact graphic/header block near the top left. The HTML shows a smaller logo/header treatment with extra text placement and a horizontal rule, changing the appearance and placement of the graphic area.

findings.description: Title and header formatting differ noticeably from the PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title is smaller and centered above the table. In the HTML, the title is larger, left-aligned, and separated by a thick horizontal rule, altering the original emphasis and alignment.

findings.description: The HTML introduces fabricated wrapper and styling content not present in the PDF, including a synthetic HTML title and extensive CSS/theme text.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<title>Converted Document</title>' and CSS comments/text such as 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS', font imports, and style rules. None of this textual content appears in the PDF image, which only shows the USDA logo/header, title, table, and footnotes.

findings.description: The HTML adds a 'page-header' line duplicating and restructuring the agency identification beyond the PDF layout.
findings.evidence:
PDF header visually shows the USDA logo with 'United States Department of Agriculture' as part of the header area. HTML separately includes '

United States Department of Agriculture

' under a figure plus additional wrapper labels like 'page-header'/'page-footer' that are not in the PDF.

findings.description: The HTML title omits the footnote marker '/1' from the source document title.
findings.evidence: PDF title: 'Reported Case and Procedural Error Rates /1'. HTML h1: 'Reported Case and Procedural Error Rates /1' is correct, but the document metadata title is 'Converted Document', which is inaccurate relative to the source title.
